query: FAA Boeing resume issuing certificates new airplanes 737 MAX 787 limited delegation
a_issues: Content A includes one strong official FAA result and one relevant secondary result, but its top result is a non-authoritative site about a later July 2026 development that could distract from the question about limited delegation. It also lacks the key detail visible in the FAA statement snippet that Boeing and the FAA would alternate issuing certificates on alternating weeks. One result is unrelated (Boeing 2020 MAX operations statement).
b_issues: Content B includes lower-quality/social results (Facebook posts) and one later Investing.com result about full resumption for all 737 MAX and 787s, which is beyond the limited-delegation event. However, the top FAA result contains the core facts needed and is accurate.
reasoning: Both sets contain the crucial FAA statement, but Content B better enables a correct answer because its top result directly states the facts matching the query: the FAA will allow limited delegation to Boeing to issue airworthiness certificates for some 737 MAX and 787 airplanes starting Sept. 29, 2025; this follows a production-quality review; Boeing and the FAA will issue certificates on alternating weeks; and Boeing had previously lost this authority for the 737 MAX in 2019 and the 787 in 2022. Content A has the FAA result too, but its surrounding results are more distracting, especially the top 2026 article about a later full resumption and an unrelated 2020 Boeing page. So B is slightly better grounded for answering the asked question correctly.
